business_review.criterion: Heal at call site only (shared loaders untouched)
Both entry points covered: by-binding miss + by-id-expired
Re-read by binding after heal (collapses both paths)
skip_auth_resolution bypasses enterprise loop AND legacy fallback
user_id=None for connect; real user_id for probe (load-bearing asymmetry)
Lazy import of MCPToolkitService (avoids import cycle)
Fallback: original raising loaders unchanged — same 400 on total miss
Infra errors propagate as-is (not converted to 400)
ensure_... returns None on all failure paths

standards_review.notes: All commits follow EPMCDME-13049: Capital description format
All new public methods have explicit parameter and return types
No new source files created; modified files already carry Apache 2.0 headers
No new log statements; no credentials or tokens logged
Discovery goes through run_mcp_auth_parallel_discovery_probe with allowed_private_networks and enforce_https

findings.problem: When cls.get_instance() raises RuntimeError (singleton uninitialized), _default = None is silently passed as default_toolkit_service. This propagates as AttributeError wrapped in MCPToolLoadException, which _build_discovery_candidate_from_challenge receives and returns None for (cause is not httpx.HTTPStatusError), causing ensure_... to silently return None with no log entry. The # type: ignore[assignment] comment labels this path as test-only, but there is no production guard preventing it.
findings.impact: If the singleton is ever uninitialized during a production request (startup race, misconfiguration), the heal silently skips and the user gets a 400 with no operator-visible trace. The silent failure makes production diagnosis impossible.
findings.recommendation: Add logger.warning before the inner try block when _default is None. Preserves correct fallback behavior (400 per spec) while making the silent skip observable in production logs.

rationale: 13/13 spec acceptance criteria satisfied. All three lenses completed (blind, edge-case, acceptance). One major patch finding: silent RuntimeError swallow in ensure_discovered_snapshot_for_server lacks a log warning. Three findings deferred: TOCTOU re-read race on total miss (acceptable per spec same-400 guarantee), uncaught coroutine in _run_discovery_probe_and_collect_failures (pre-existing, not introduced here), config_error auth_failure silent fallthrough (correct 400 behavior). Four dismissed: intentional infra error propagation, user_id=None credential-less connect by design, upstream guards on _mcp_server_from_config, incorrect blind finding about conditional re-fetch.
